agribusiness.- Cocoa processing arbitrice: With bean prices at 20-month lows, margins for local grinding, butter/powder production, and chocolate manufacturing are widening — Ghanaian processors should be locking in supply now
agribusiness.- Rubber supply gap: Natural rubber at 234.40 cents/kg, up 46.50% YoY and at highs since January 2017 — West African rubber plantations (Liberia, Nigeria, Côte d'Ivoire) are well-positioned to capture demand displaced by Thai/Indonesian weather disruptions
agribusiness.- Rice import substitution: ECOWAS's $5bn annual rice import bill remains the region's most bankable agri-investment thesis — milling, seed systems, and warehouse infrastructure are all underinvested
agribusiness.- Cocoa price trajectory: Trading Economics forecasts $3,862/t by end-Q2 and $3,259/t in 12 months — further declines will pressure COCOBOD's ability to maintain the GH¢2,587/bag producer price
agribusiness.- West African monsoon onset: Thailand and Indonesia face weather disruptions; West Africa's June–September cocoa mid-crop harvest will be the next major supply signal
agribusiness.- Ghana cedi at GH\u00a211.79/$: Further depreciation raises input costs (fertiliser, machinery) for farmers even as export commodity prices in dollars soften
agribusiness.- COCOBOD 2025/26 pricing policy: Whether the GH¢2,587/bag (approximately $2,190/t at current rates) remains competitive if the cedi strengthens or global prices recover
